title: cyclanthaceae
intro: The Cyclanthaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants. This family is composed of approximately 240 species, commonly known as "coyol" or "burití", which are native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Cyclanthaceae family is characterized by its unique, palm-like appearance and its ability to thrive in a variety of environments, from tropical forests to open grasslands. The plants within this family are often valued for their edible fruits and seeds, which are rich in nutrients and have been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
